name: Mauritius (Republic of Mauritius)
summary: Mauritius is an Indian Ocean mixed-legal-system republic structurally distinctive globally — combining French civil-law substantive heritage (via 17th-19th century French colonial inheritance — Code Napoléon 1804 adopted) with English common-law procedural inheritance (post-1810 British administration). This Quebec/Saint-Lucia pattern is structurally distinctive within the African corpus — Mauritius shares the French-civil-substantive + English-common-law-procedural hybrid. Family-law framework operates under the Civil Code 1804 (Code Napoléon) supplemented by the Divorce and Judicial Separation Act 1986, the Maintenance Act, the Child Protection Act 1994, and the Children's Act 2020 (Act 13/2020). Parental authority (autorité parentale) and child custody are governed by Civil Code arts. 371-387 and Children's Act 2020. The Supreme Court of Mauritius is the apex domestic court for civil and criminal matters; final appellate jurisdiction was retained with the Judicial Committee of the Privy Council in London. Family-law matters are heard at first instance in the Family Division of the Supreme Court and Family Court (established 2017). Psychology profession is regulated through the Allied Health Professionals Council. Mauritius is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label; courts operate substantively under the best-interests-of-the-child standard codified in Children's Act s. 4. Mauritius acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 February 1993 — among earliest African Hague accessions in the corpus.

statutory_framework.title: Children's Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Children's Act codifying best-interests-of-the-child principle (s. 4), parental responsibility, custody, and children's protection provisions.

statutory_framework.title: Civil Code — Parental authority
statutory_framework.relevance: Federal Civil Code drawing on French Napoleonic Code substantive heritage adopted 1804. Arts. 371-387 govern autorité parentale.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of Mauritius
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic court for civil and criminal matters.

apex_courts.name: Judicial Committee of the Privy Council
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Mauritius.

professional_regulators.name: Allied Health Professionals Council, Mauritius
professional_regulators.role: Federal regulator of allied health professionals including clinical psychology.

anonymisation_convention: Mauritian family-court decisions are anonymised per Supreme Court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: French-colonisation + Isle-de-France-framework
key_developments.description: French colonisation of Mauritius (then Isle de France) 1715 by Guillaume Dufresne d'Arsel — substantively distinctive Indian Ocean French-colonial-+-Code-Napoléon-substantive-heritage framework. Subsequent 1721 Compagnie française des Indes orientales settlement. Foundational pre-1810-British-acquisition framework affecting subsequent constitutional-administrative-trajectory and French-civil-law substantive heritage.

key_developments.title: British colonial administration + Civil-Code-retained-+-Code-Napoléon-preservation framework
key_developments.description: Mauritius transferred from French to British colonial administration 3 December 1810 — substantively distinctive globally Civil-Code-substantive-heritage-retained-with-English-common-law-procedural-inheritance framework. Foundational substantive 216-year Quebec-pattern-French-civil-+-English-common-law-procedural-hybrid framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Independence + JCPC-retention framework
key_developments.description: Mauritius achieved independence from the United Kingdom 12 March 1968 — substantively distinctive globally only-African-corpus-jurisdiction-with-JCPC-final-appellate-jurisdiction framework. Retained JCPC as final appellate court. Foundational substantive 58-year independent-republic-+-JCPC framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Mauritius ratifies UN Convention on the Rights of the Child framework
key_developments.description: Mauritius ratified UNCRC 26 July 1990 — substantively significant child-rights framework integration. Foundational best-interests-of-the-child framework integration with subsequent 1994 Child Protection Act + 2020 Children's Act substantive doctrine.

key_developments.title: Mauritius becomes a Republic + Westminster-republic-framework
key_developments.description: Mauritius became a Republic 12 March 1992 — substantively distinctive Indian Ocean Westminster-republic-framework with President as Head of State replacing British monarch. Foundational substantive 33-year Mauritian-Republic framework affecting subsequent constitutional-democratic-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Hague Convention 1980 accession + earliest-African-Hague-accession framework
key_developments.description: Mauritius acceded to the Hague Convention 1980 effective 1 February 1993 — substantively distinctive globally among-earliest-African-Hague-accessions framework. Foundational substantive cross-border-jurisdiction-practice framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Child Protection Act + child-protection-codification framework
key_developments.description: Federal Child Protection Act 1994 enacted establishing child-protection framework — substantively significant Indian Ocean Mauritian-child-protection-codification framework. Foundational substantive child-protection framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Family Court + specialised-Family-Court framework
key_developments.description: Specialised Family Court established within Supreme Court framework 2017 — substantively distinctive Indian Ocean Mauritian-specialised-Family-Court framework. Foundational substantive specialised-family-court framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory affecting family-law-implementation.

key_developments.title: Children's Act 2020 + best-interests-codification framework
key_developments.description: Federal Children's Act (Act 13/2020) 2020 enacted codifying best-interests-of-the-child principle (s. 4), parental responsibility, custody, and children's protection — substantively significant Indian Ocean Mauritian-Children's-Act-codification framework. Foundational substantive children's-rights framework persisting through subsequent reform-trajectory.

key_developments.title: Ombudsperson for Children + Ramgoolam framework + Chagos-Archipelago-pre-transfer framework
key_developments.description: Ombudsperson for Children institutional position addressing PA-related issues within Children's Act 2020 + Civil Code framework. Pravind Jugnauth (MSM) continuing premiership through 2024 then Navin Ramgoolam (MMM-Labour) election November 2024 substantively significant political-transition context. Chagos Archipelago pre-transfer framework substantively significant institutional context establishing trajectory toward 2024 UK-Mauritius sovereignty transfer agreement. Substantively significant Indian Ocean institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Supreme Court of Mauritius + JCPC — best-interests-of-the-child substantive register + continuing-Chagos-Archipelago-sovereignty context framework
key_developments.description: Supreme Court of Mauritius and JCPC continue to develop best-interests-of-the-child jurisprudence under Children's Act 2020 s. 4 + Civil Code 1804 arts. 371-387 + Hague Convention 1980 framework in custody disputes within continuing Chagos Archipelago sovereignty transfer context (2024 UK-Mauritius sovereignty transfer agreement). Substantive analysis without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label adoption.

structural_findings: Mauritius operates a structurally distinctive mixed-legal-system framework — French civil-law substantive heritage (Code Napoléon 1804 retained at 1810 British transition) + English common-law procedural inheritance. Unique pattern in African corpus; shared with Quebec, Saint Lucia within the corpus.
Hague Convention 1980 accession 1993 places Mauritius as among earliest African Hague accessions within the corpus.

Judicial Committee of the Privy Council retention as final appellate court is structurally distinctive within Africa — Mauritius is the only African corpus jurisdiction with JCPC final-appellate jurisdiction.
Trilingual de facto framework (English official + French + Mauritian Creole) is structurally distinctive within the Indian Ocean cluster.

sources.title: Judicial Committee of the Privy Council

editorial_notes: Mauritius jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — deepened 2026-06-09 from 6 to 10 key_developments with full French-colonisation-1715-to-continuing-Chagos-Archipelago-sovereignty trajectory: 1715-French-colonisation-Isle-de-France + 1810-British-colonial-administration + 1968-Independence + 1990-Mauritius-UNCRC-ratification + 1992-Mauritius-becomes-Republic + 1993-Hague-Convention-accession + 1994-Child-Protection-Act + 2017-Family-Court + 2020-Children's-Act + 2024-Supreme-Court-+-JCPC-+-Chagos-sovereignty-context.

Mixed-legal-system Indian Ocean (French civil-law substantive Code Napoléon + English common-law procedural + JCPC final-appellate + Children's Act 2020 + Hague Convention 1980 accession 1993). Quebec/Saint-Lucia pattern (French-civil-substantive + English-common-law-procedural hybrid) globally distinctive within African corpus + only African corpus jurisdiction with JCPC final-appellate jurisdiction + among earliest African Hague accessions 1993 + continuing Chagos Archipelago sovereignty transfer context.

PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator — substantive best-interests-of-the-child analysis under Children's Act 2020 s. 4 + Civil Code 1804 arts. 371-387 + Hague Convention 1980 framework without doctrinal 'parental alienation' label.

Joins Indian-Ocean + mixed-legal-system + Quebec-pattern-French-civil-English-procedural-globally-distinctive + only-African-corpus-jurisdiction-with-JCPC-final-appellate-jurisdiction-globally-distinctive + earliest-African-Hague-accessions-1993 + French-colonisation-Isle-de-France-1715-Code-Napoléon-substantive-heritage + British-colonial-administration-1810-Civil-Code-retained + Independence-1968-Westminster-realm + Mauritius-becomes-Republic-1992-Westminster-republic + Family-Court-2017-specialised + Children's-Act-2020-Act-13/2020 + continuing-Chagos-Archipelago-sovereignty-transfer-2024-UK-Mauritius-agreement clusters within the corpus.
